implemented_rules: Mapped implementation scope to planner constraints: dual mode (watch/poll), OAuth2 refresh, checkpoint+dedup, attachment policy, retry/backoff, and trigger health surfaces.
Preserved architecture guardrail intent: no transport-layer business logic, idempotent event emission path, and additive Gmail-specific payload contract.
Defined persistence shape for config/state/dedup/dead-letter compatibility with existing trigger lifecycle.
known_limitations: No repository writes were possible in this execution because filesystem sandbox is read-only (write operations return 'Operation not permitted').
No migrations, handlers, workers, or adapters were actually created/modified in code.
No compile/test run was possible for this feature since implementation was not applied.
areas_needing_tests: End-to-end activation flow with OAuth2 credential linkage and automatic token refresh.
Watch mode Pub/Sub ingestion idempotency with duplicate/out-of-order notifications.
Polling mode incremental cursoring, overlap windows, and dedup across restarts.
Invalid historyId recovery path and bounded resync/backfill behavior.
Attachment policy enforcement (none/metadata/download), size limits, and MIME edge cases.
Retry/backoff classification for 429/5xx/network vs terminal auth/scope errors.
State/checkpoint transactional ordering to avoid missed or duplicated emissions.
integration_points: Credential/secrets manager for encrypted refresh-token storage and rotation handling.
Gmail API adapter endpoints: users.watch/stop, history.list, messages.get, attachments.get.
Scheduler/queue system for poll cadence, watch renewal, and retriable jobs.
Webhook ingress for Pub/Sub push notifications with trigger routing/authentication.
Persistent store tables for gmail_trigger_configs, gmail_trigger_state, gmail_dedup, gmail_dead_letters.
Trigger runtime/event bus for emitting normalized Gmail trigger events.
